---
title: "Get Started"
subtitle: "Install *Gribouille* and build a real plot, one layer at a time."
page-navigation: false
toc: true
engine: markdown
---

## Prerequisites

- [Typst](https://typst.app) {{< var typst.series >}} or later.
- Familiarity with Typst `#import` syntax.
  No prior experience with the grammar of graphics is assumed, but if you have used [`ggplot2`](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org) in R or [`plotnine`](https://plotnine.org) in Python the mental model will feel familiar.

## Installation

Import _Gribouille_ from Typst Universe, or from a local clone of the repository.

### From Typst Universe

```typst
#import "@preview/gribouille:{{< env VERSION >}}": *
```

### From a local clone

```typst
#import "./path/to/gribouille/lib.typ": *
```

This imports every public symbol so you can call `plot`, `aes`, `geom-point`, and friends directly.

## Tutorial: build a penguins plot, one layer at a time

The library ships the [Palmer Penguins](https://allisonhorst.github.io/palmerpenguins/) dataset as the `penguins` symbol.
We will build a single figure end to end, adding one grammar concept per step so you can see the role each piece plays.

### 1. Data and aesthetics

Every plot starts with a dataset and a [`aes`](../reference/core/aes.qmd) mapping that binds column names to visual channels.
At minimum [`geom-point`](../reference/geoms/geom-point.qmd) needs `x` and `y`.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-1.svg"
//| alt: "Scatter of penguin body mass against flipper length, all points the same colour."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(x: "flipper-len", y: "body-mass"),
  layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt),),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 2. Encode a third variable through colour

Add `colour: "species"` to the mapping and the points split visually by group.
Every aesthetic mapping is independent of the geom, so the same colour scale will apply to whatever layers you stack on top.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-2.svg"
//| alt: "Scatter of body mass against flipper length, points coloured by species."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
  ),
  layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.85),),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 3. Stack a second layer (`geom-smooth`)

Layers stack in order.
Adding a [`geom-smooth`](../reference/geoms/geom-smooth.qmd) draws one fitted line per colour group because the `colour` aesthetic is inherited from the plot-level mapping.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-3.svg"
//| alt: "Per-species scatter with linear fits and 95% confidence ribbons."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 4. Pick a custom colour palette

[`scale-colour-discrete`](../reference/scales/scale-colour-discrete.qmd) replaces the default palette.
A matching [`scale-fill-discrete`](../reference/scales/scale-fill-discrete.qmd) keeps the smooth ribbon's fill in sync with the points.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-4.svg"
//| alt: "Same scatter as step 3 with a colour-blind-friendly palette."
#let cb = (
  rgb("#0072B2"),  // blue
  rgb("#D55E00"),  // vermillion
  rgb("#009E73"),  // bluish green
)

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-fill-discrete(palette: cb),
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 5. Split into small multiples with `facet-wrap`

[`facet-wrap`](../reference/facets/facet-wrap.qmd) lays one panel per level of a discrete column.
The same scales apply to every panel, so the species clusters can be compared across islands.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-5.svg"
//| alt: "Three side-by-side panels, one per island, sharing colour and axis scales."
#let cb = (
  rgb("#0072B2"),
  rgb("#D55E00"),
  rgb("#009E73"),
)

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-fill-discrete(palette: cb),
  ),
  facet: facet-wrap("island"),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 6. Polish with `theme()` and `labs()`

[`theme-minimal`](../reference/themes/theme-minimal.qmd) drops most of the chrome.
[`labs`](../reference/labs/labs.qmd) sets the title block and renames every axis and legend.
The single [`theme`](../reference/themes/theme.qmd) call further tweaks the title surface.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-6.svg"
//| alt: "Polished version of the faceted scatter with title block, axis labels, and minimal theme."
#let cb = (
  rgb("#0072B2"),
  rgb("#D55E00"),
  rgb("#009E73"),
)

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-fill-discrete(palette: cb),
  ),
  facet: facet-wrap("island"),
  labs: labs(
    title: "Penguin Body Mass Scales with Flipper Length",
    subtitle: "Same trend on every island, even though sample sizes differ",
    x: "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y: "Body Mass (g)",
    colour: "Species",
    fill: "Species",
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(
    plot-title: element-text(size: 12pt, weight: "bold")
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 7. Format tick labels with `format-comma`

Tick labels accept any formatter callable.
[`scale-y-continuous`](../reference/scales/scale-y-continuous.qmd) with `labels: format-comma()` renders body mass as `3,000`, `4,000`, ... instead of bare digits.
Sibling formatters [`format-percent`](../reference/helpers/format-percent.qmd), [`format-currency`](../reference/helpers/format-currency.qmd), and [`format-scientific`](../reference/helpers/format-scientific.qmd) share the same call shape.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-7.svg"
//| alt: "Polished faceted scatter whose body-mass tick labels carry thousands separators."
#let cb = (
  rgb("#0072B2"),
  rgb("#D55E00"),
  rgb("#009E73"),
)

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-fill-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-y-continuous(labels: format-comma()),
  ),
  facet: facet-wrap("island"),
  labs: labs(
    title: "Penguin Body Mass Scales with Flipper Length",
    subtitle: "Same trend on every island, even though sample sizes differ",
    x: "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y: "Body Mass (g)",
    colour: "Species",
    fill: "Species",
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(
    plot-title: element-text(size: 12pt, weight: "bold")
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 8. Render Typst markup in the subtitle with `element-typst`

[`element-typst`](../reference/themes/element-typst.qmd) is a drop-in replacement for [`element-text`](../reference/themes/element-text.qmd) that evaluates plain strings as Typst markup.
Wiring it to the `plot-subtitle` theme surface lets the subtitle mix prose with math: anything between `$ $` becomes a rendered formula while the rest reads as ordinary text.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-8.svg"
//| alt: "Step 7 plot with a subtitle whose linear-fit formula renders as Typst math."
#let cb = (
  rgb("#0072B2"),
  rgb("#D55E00"),
  rgb("#009E73"),
)
#let n = penguins.species.len()

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-fill-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-y-continuous(labels: format-comma()),
  ),
  facet: facet-wrap("island"),
  labs: labs(
    title: "Penguin Body Mass Scales with Flipper Length",
    subtitle: "Linear fit: $hat(y) = beta_0 + beta_1 dot.c x$ for $n = " + str(n) + "$ birds",
    x: "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y: "Body Mass (g)",
    colour: "Species",
    fill: "Species",
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(
    plot-title: element-text(size: 12pt, weight: "bold"),
    plot-subtitle: element-typst(size: 9pt),
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

### 9. Annotate inside the panel with `geom-typst`

[`geom-typst`](../reference/geoms/geom-typst.qmd) is the Typst-markup sibling of [`geom-text`](../reference/geoms/geom-text.qmd): every value of the `label` aesthetic renders as Typst at the row's `(x, y)`.
The annotation layer carries one row per `(species, island)` cell so each `$N_"species" = ...$` label rides the colour scale defined in step 4.
Every row also carries an `island` value so [`facet-wrap`](../reference/facets/facet-wrap.qmd) routes it to the right panel.

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "step-9.svg"
//| alt: "Step 8 plot with per-species sample-size labels rendered as Typst math inside each facet panel."
#let cb = (
  rgb("#0072B2"),
  rgb("#D55E00"),
  rgb("#009E73"),
)
#let n = penguins.species.len()

#let species-counts = (
  (island: "Torgersen", flipper-len: 172, body-mass: 6200, species: "Adelie",    label: $N_"Adelie" = 52$),
  (island: "Biscoe",    flipper-len: 172, body-mass: 6200, species: "Adelie",    label: $N_"Adelie" = 44$),
  (island: "Dream",     flipper-len: 172, body-mass: 6200, species: "Adelie",    label: $N_"Adelie" = 56$),
  (island: "Dream",     flipper-len: 172, body-mass: 5700, species: "Chinstrap", label: $N_"Chinstrap" = 68$),
  (island: "Biscoe",    flipper-len: 172, body-mass: 5700, species: "Gentoo",    label: $N_"Gentoo" = 124$),
)

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.6),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
    geom-typst(
      data: species-counts,
      mapping: aes(
        x: "flipper-len",
        y: "body-mass",
        colour: "species",
        label: "label",
      ),
      inherit-aes: false,
      anchor: "west",
      size: 9pt,
    ),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-fill-discrete(palette: cb),
    scale-y-continuous(labels: format-comma()),
  ),
  facet: facet-wrap("island"),
  labs: labs(
    title: "Penguin Body Mass Scales with Flipper Length",
    subtitle: "Linear fit: $hat(y) = beta_0 + beta_1 dot.c x$ for $n = " + str(n) + "$ birds",
    x: "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y: "Body Mass (g)",
    colour: "Species",
    fill: "Species",
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(
    plot-title: element-text(size: 12pt, weight: "bold"),
    plot-subtitle: element-typst(size: 9pt),
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

## The grammar in one diagram

Every plot you build composes the same pieces.
`plot()` wraps a stack of layers, each sheet sitting on the one below, from data and mapping at the foundation up through theme and labels.

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| output-filename: "grammar.svg"
//| style: "display: block; margin-inline: auto;"
//| alt: "A plot() container surrounding six stacked parallax sheets representing the grammar layers, from bottom to top: data plus aes(), layer (geom plus stat plus position), scale-*, coord-*, facet-*, and theme plus labs."
#import "@preview/cetz:0.5.0": canvas, draw

#let ink = if _typst_render_foreground != none {
  _typst_render_foreground
} else {
  rgb("#1f2328")
}
#let accent = rgb("#3366FF")

#canvas(length: 1cm, {
  import draw: *

  set-style(content: (frame: none))

  let w = 7
  let h = 0.9
  let s = 0.6
  let gap = 0.15
  let n = 6
  let pad = 0.5
  let total-h = n * (h + gap) - gap

  let palette = (
    rgb("#E69F00"),
    rgb("#009E73"),
    rgb("#56B4E9"),
    rgb("#D55E00"),
    rgb("#0072B2"),
    rgb("#F4B740"),
  )

  line(
    (-pad, -pad),
    (w + pad, -pad),
    (w + pad + s, total-h + pad),
    (-pad + s, total-h + pad),
    close: true,
    stroke: 0.8pt + accent,
    fill: accent.transparentize(92%),
  )
  content(
    ((w + 2 * s) / 2, total-h + pad + 0.35),
    align(center, text(size: 9pt, weight: "bold", fill: accent)[`plot()`]),
  )

  let sheet(i, name, sub: none) = {
    let y = i * (h + gap)
    let col = palette.at(i)
    line(
      (0, y),
      (w, y),
      (w + s, y + h),
      (s, y + h),
      close: true,
      stroke: 0.8pt + col,
      fill: col.transparentize(80%),
    )
    let body = if sub == none {
      text(size: 9pt, fill: ink)[#name]
    } else {
      text(size: 9pt, fill: ink)[
        #name \
        #text(size: 7.5pt)[#sub]
      ]
    }
    content((w / 2 + s / 2, y + h / 2), align(center, body))
  }

  sheet(0, [data + `aes()`])
  sheet(1, [layer], sub: [(geom + stat + position)])
  sheet(2, [`scale-*`])
  sheet(3, [`coord-*`])
  sheet(4, [`facet-*`])
  sheet(5, [theme + labs])
})
```

## Next steps

- Read the [Reference](../reference/index.qmd) to see every function the library exposes.
- Browse the [Examples](../examples/index.qmd) gallery for idiomatic usage.
- Check the [Changelog](../changelog.qmd) for recent changes.
